frame_analyses.description: Initial view of wooden FLS trainer with marked circular pattern on gauze suspended between clamps. Green needle driver visible on left entering field.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup complete; gauze secured between clamps; marked circle visible (appears to be approximately 4cm diameter).

frame_analyses.description: Green needle driver with scissors beginning initial cut on superior aspect of marked circle. Black grasper positioned to stabilize gauze on opposite side.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors approach appears perpendicular to gauze; initial cut placement on marked line; good tension control.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting progressing along upper-right quadrant of circle. Green scissors making controlled advance along marked line; black grasper providing dynamic tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth scissor advancement; gauze tension maintained; cutting appears to follow marked line within acceptable tolerance.

frame_analyses.description: Continuing around right side of circle. Scissors positioned at approximately 3 o'clock position on circle. Gauze held taut by opposite grasper.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument control; consistent line following; no visible deviation >2mm from mark.

frame_analyses.description: Cut progressing to lower-right quadrant (approximately 4-5 o'clock). Scissors advancing smoothly; gauze remains secured and under tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Technique remains consistent; line adherence good; steady instrument advancement.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors at bottom of circle (approximately 6 o'clock). Cutting continues with steady advance. Black grasper provides counter-tension on opposite side.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth transition through bottom quadrant; maintained cutting precision.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors now on left side of circle (approximately 9 o'clock position). Gauze remains fully secured between clamps; no detachment observed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Continuing around circle; transition to counterclockwise phase smooth; line following consistent.

frame_analyses.description: Cut advancing through upper-left quadrant (approximately 10-11 o'clock). Nearly complete circumference cut; small remaining segment visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Approach to closure point; instrument control maintained; final segment preparation.

frame_analyses.description: Final closing cut executing junction of start and end points. Scissors advancing to complete circumference. Gauze still firmly held in clamps.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final segment cut with precision; junction point appears clean; no over-cutting into clamp area.

frame_analyses.description: Cut completed; circular specimen fully separated from surrounding gauze. Specimen appears intact with clean edges. Gauze remains secured in clamps.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion achieved; circular specimen cleanly extracted; no gauze detachment from clamps; pattern appears to follow marked line within tolerance.

frame_analyses.description: Post-completion view. Circular specimen suspended; remaining gauze intact in clamps. Instruments withdrawn from field.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean completion with no complications; specimen integrity maintained.

phase_timings.benchmark_comparison: Setup phase; preparation time within normal range

penalties.description: Estimated maximum deviation from marked line at approximately 3 o'clock position (right quadrant): approximately 1-1.5mm lateral deviation observed. Within acceptable tolerance of 2mm.

gauze_integrity.notes: Gauze remained fully secured throughout procedure; no stress or tearing observed at clamp points.

confidence_rationale: Completion time is clearly readable from timestamped frames. Cutting line deviation estimated from oblique camera angles—maximum deviation appears to stay within 2mm tolerance at all cardinal points, though exact measurements are subject to camera distortion. Gauze integrity and specimen completeness are unambiguous. Overall confidence is moderate-to-good; primary limitation is precise mm-level deviation measurement from video angles.
cannot_determine: Exact deviation at each cardinal point to 0.1mm precision due to video camera angle and magnification variability
Whether cutting edge might be slightly ragged at microscopic level—video resolution limits fine edge assessment
technique_summary: Competent intermediate performance with clean, controlled cutting throughout. Trainee demonstrated smooth instrument management, consistent line following within tolerance, and successful specimen extraction without gauze detachment. Completion in 155 seconds is faster than intermediate benchmark (90–180s) but slower than expert range (60–90s), placing performance solidly in intermediate-to-good category.
strengths: Smooth, continuous circular cutting with consistent pace
Excellent gauze tension control throughout procedure
No gauze detachment or stress on clamp points
Clean junction of start and end points
Efficient instrument economy—no excess motion or repositioning needed
improvement_suggestions: Reduce setup/initial cut phase by 5–10 seconds to move toward expert benchmark
Maintain current line-following precision while gradually increasing cutting speed
Practice maintaining perpendicular scissors orientation throughout 360-degree arc to minimize deviation variance
